Convinced that "God is not partial", we belong to a spiritual brotherhood that is really internationally, free of racist and political obstacles. Although we are usually, we make up a unique people.

English: Walk on the road to life.
This is the story of a little boy who lived with his father and his stepmother. The stepmother had a daughter, the boy's half-sister.

review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: Indeed, the stepmother was very unkind to the poor boy. One day, his stepmother sent him to the river to wash the dishes.

review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: And the latter had the misfortune to break a spoon without wanting it. He was frightened and began to cry, because he knew what would happen to him when he returned home.

review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: When he arrived home, he told his stepmother that a spoon was broken. His stepmother, furious, chased him out of the house, and told him to come back only with a new spoon.
Medumba: Ngelañ a na’ kum la’ là, a tshob nzwi tàab mbe a be’ ta’ djo’. Nzwi tàab ya ntù, nta’ i ndà, ntshob yi mbe a nèn diag du’ là njen kà mbèn ndà.
review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: The boy left the house, without water, without food, and without knowing where he should go. He headed for the forest, walking for days and nights, eating whatever he could find. He hardly slept at all.
Medumba: Yen mènnshum nà tum ndà nène, ke ntse, ke jujù, ke ne ndèn mba dù’ z’a tshwèt nèn nà. A lo’ manze fèn, nzin mbwôgtshù bo tèttswe’ yàme, nke njù ju z’a tèt là. A na’ ke nke nzi yàme.
review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: One day, while walking, he saw a house in the distance in the forest and decided to go there. He went in, saw an old woman all dirty, greeted her, and asked her if he could rest. The old woman, all flabbergasted and surprised, asked him what he was doing all alone in the forest.
Medumba: A na’ be zin ta’ lé’, njen ta’ ba’ ta njam fèn, ndo’ mfi’ nenèn bwe. A kum bwe, ntsho ntùm ba’ là, njen ta’ njwèn mènnzwi ndù à lôtsile, a tsha’d’é, mbètt’é a be z’a ku’ni nefide wul i là. Njwèn mènnzwi tshàm neshu, mbètt’é ju z’a tshwèt ngù tù’ i ntùm mfèn nà.
review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: While crying, the boy told her his story. The woman took him by the hand and tried to calm him down. She gave him what food she had. The boy then asked her if he could go to the river and get him some water to drink.
Medumba: Boa ntsenya miag mi, mènnshum soñ i ze tshô. Mènnzwi lo’ bu i, nkagtùn nefide ntù i. A fà juju z’a na’ ngù ndà là yi. Mènnshum bètt’é be z’a ku’ni netsho’ ntse yi mà koñntse là.
review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: The old lady said yes, so he took a calabash and went to the river. He returned with the calabash full of water. Afterwards, he swept the woman's house, made food, washed her feet, in short, he tried to put the old woman's house in order.
Medumba: Njwèn mènnzwi bam, mènnshum lo’ ta’ tam nka, nèn mà nkoñntse. A be bèn bo tam nka ndù à dun ntse. Be nèn mbwe, a bé’de tèt ndà yen mènnzwi là, nabte juju, nsôg nkum i, nabte ndà njwèn mènnzwi à làn.
review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: After he had rested enough, it was time to leave, because he had to find his spoon. The boy got up and said goodbye to the old lady. But she held him back and gave him some instructions.
Medumba: Ngelañ a miagde nefide wul i là, ngelañ nenèn kum, nummbe a na’ bètte a fon djo’ a na’ mbi là. Mènnshum lo nsi, ndé’de njwèn mènnzwi. Là, a tshùb i mbèn mfà bontsit ntag yi.
review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: You see my little one, she said to him, here you are who leaves to look for the spoon. Listen carefully to what I am going to tell you. When you walk, at a certain moment, you will see six balls on the ground, all extremely fragile. You don't have to be afraid.
Medumba: A tshob yi mbe, o lèn ké nshum àm, o là z’o tshwèt nèn diag djo’ là. Ju’ ju ze me tshob wù là kua’ mebwô. Ngelañ oa’ be nke nzin nà, mo’ ngelañ, o yen mbum ntôg ze b’a tshét là num tsha’a. Kà bwôg.
review_reason: Long entry; not a basic concept text without splitting.

English: The first three will say to you: "Take us, take us". and the other three will say: "Don't take us, don't take us". So what you will do is you will take the last three, do you understand?
Medumba: Tse tat a’ tshob wù ndù: “lo’ yage, lo” yage”, tsemo’ tat tshob wù ndù: “kà lo” yage, kà lo’ yage”. Ndùlà ju z’o ghù là b’a yenni, oa’ lo’ mbum tat tse njam, o ju’ mebwô ké?

English: He greeted the lady, and said goodbye. He left the house and walked for hours, and then suddenly he saw the balls.
